questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

As artificial intelligence becomes more capable, society must develop new frameworks to _____________ the ethical risks associated with autonomous decision-making systems.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2026년 국가직 1번, 2025년 지방직 1번 / 핵심 개념: 시사 어휘 - AI 윤리 관련 빈칸] 정답은 ①번. 'ethical risks(윤리적 위험)'를 '완화하다(mitigate)'가 적절. amplify(증폭하다)는 위험을 키우는 것이라 반대. neglect(방치하다)는 책임 회피로 부적절. celebrate(축하하다)는 위험에 맞지 않음. mitigate는 시사 영어에서 climate change mitigation, risk mitigation 등으로 매우 빈출.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

The whistleblower's _____________ allegations exposed corruption that had remained hidden within the organization for over a decade.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 지방직 2번 / 핵심 개념: 시사 어휘 - 내부고발 관련] 정답은 ②번. 'exposed corruption(부패를 폭로했다)'은 폭로가 사실로 인정되었음을 함의하므로 'credible(신뢰할 만한)'이 적절. groundless(근거 없는), ambiguous(모호한), irrelevant(무관한)는 모두 부패 폭로의 효력과 어긋남. credible은 credibility, credentials와 같은 어근으로 시사 영어에서 매우 빈출.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

It is essential that every citizen _____________ aware of the changes to the new tax regulations before they take effect next month.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2023년 국가직 6번 / 핵심 개념: 이성적 판단의 형용사 + that + S + (should) + 동사원형] 정답은 ③번. 'essential, important, necessary, vital, imperative' 같은 이성적 판단(당위) 형용사 뒤 that절은 'should + 동사원형'을 쓰며 should 생략 가능. 따라서 'be aware'. is, was는 직설법으로 부적절. being은 분사. 제안·요구·명령 동사뿐 아니라 이성적 판단 형용사도 같은 규칙이 적용됨을 반드시 기억.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

[메신저 대화]
Jiwon: Hey, did you submit your tax return yet? The deadline is next Friday.
Minsu: Not yet, I'm still gathering my documents. _____________?
Jiwon: You can do it online through the government tax portal. It's pretty straightforward if you have all your receipts ready.
Minsu: Oh, that's good to know. Do I need to install any special software?
Jiwon: No, you just need your digital ID for authentication.
Minsu: Thanks! That makes things a lot easier.

choices.text: When did the deadline get extended
Where is the nearest tax office
How much do I owe in taxes this year
What's the easiest way to file it

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2026년 국가직 4번, 2025년 지방직 5번 / 핵심 개념: 메신저 빈칸 - 시사 주제(전자 정부 서비스)] 정답은 ④번. 빈칸 직후 'You can do it online through the government tax portal(정부 세무 포털에서 온라인으로 할 수 있다)'은 '방법'에 대한 답변이므로 'What's the easiest way to file it(가장 쉬운 신고 방법은?)'이 정답. 세액은 응답과 무관. 마감 연장은 첫 줄 'deadline is next Friday'와 모순. 세무서 위치를 물었다면 응답이 'It's at ~' 형태였을 것. 신유형: 디지털 세무 신고는 공무원 실무에서 빈출.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

A: I'm thinking about switching to remote work permanently. What do you think?
B: That's a big decision. Have you considered all the implications?
A: Like what?
B: Well, _____________.
A: That's a fair point. I do find that brainstorming sessions are more productive in person.
B: Right. You might want to negotiate a hybrid arrangement instead.

choices.text: you might lose opportunities for spontaneous collaboration with colleagues
remote workers always have more job satisfaction
your commute time will become much longer
your salary will likely increase significantly

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 지방직 4번, 2026년 국가직 5번 / 핵심 개념: 시사 대화(원격근무) 빈칸 - 다음 응답이 단서] 정답은 ①번. A의 응답 'brainstorming sessions are more productive in person(브레인스토밍은 대면이 더 생산적)'에서 '대면 협업의 이점' 손실을 인정하므로, 번의 'spontaneous collaboration(즉흥적 협업) 기회 상실'이 정답. 급여 인상은 원격근무 단점과 무관. 항상 만족도 높다는 일반화는 'implications(영향)' 논의에 부합하지 않음. 원격근무면 출퇴근이 없으므로 통근 시간이 길어진다는 것은 모순.

questions.question_text:
[6~7] 다음 글을 읽고 물음에 답하시오. (공통 지문)

[웹페이지: digitalrights.gov.kr]

          (A)

The government recognizes that as artificial intelligence and digital technologies become integral to daily life, citizens need clear protections for their fundamental rights in the digital sphere.

This initiative establishes three core rights:

1. The Right to Explanation
When automated systems make significant decisions affecting you—such as loan approvals, hiring, or government services—you have the right to understand how those decisions were reached.

2. The Right to Human Review
No critical decision affecting your life, employment, or welfare should be made solely by an algorithm. You can request review by a human decision-maker.

3. The Right to Digital Privacy
Your personal data belongs to you. You have the right to know what data is collected, how it is used, and to request its deletion when no longer necessary.

How to Exercise These Rights
File your request through our online portal or visit any government service center. Responses must be provided within 30 days. If you are not satisfied with the response, you can appeal to the Digital Rights Ombudsman.

For more information, contact our 24-hour helpline at 1588-0000.

(A)에 들어갈 윗글의 제목으로 가장 적절한 것은?

choices.text: Your Digital Rights in the AI Era
How to Use Government Online Services

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 6번, 2025년 지방직 6번 / 핵심 개념: 공통 지문 제목 - 시사 주제(AI/디지털 권리)] 정답은 ②번. 전체 글이 'fundamental rights in the digital sphere(디지털 영역의 기본권)'을 소개하며 세 가지 핵심 권리(설명요구권, 인간심사권, 정보주권)를 안내. 'AI and digital technologies'의 시대에 시민의 권리를 알리는 글이므로 'Your Digital Rights in the AI Era'가 정답. 인터넷 사용 제한이 아닌 권리 보장. 행정 서비스 이용법이 아닌 권리 안내. 기술 동향이 아닌 시민 권리. 신유형: AI 거버넌스는 공무원 시험 핵심 신영역.

questions.question_text: 윗글의 내용과 일치하는 것은? (6번 공통지문 문제)

choices.text: 자동화 결정에 대한 설명 요구권은 인정되지 않는다.

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 7번, 2025년 지방직 7번 / 핵심 개념: 공통 지문 내용 일치 - 구체 정보 대조] 정답은 ③번. 'Responses must be provided within 30 days'와 정확히 일치. 'The Right to Explanation(설명요구권)'이 첫 번째 권리로 명시되어 부정과 반대. 'No critical decision ~ should be made solely by an algorithm(알고리즘 단독 결정 금지)'과 정반대. 'File your request through our online portal or visit any government service center'에서 온라인/방문 모두 가능. 함정: '또는(or)'을 단일 방법으로 오인.

questions.question_text:
[8~9] 다음 글을 읽고 물음에 답하시오. (공통 지문)

[웹페이지: cybersafety.gov]

National Cybersecurity Awareness

In an era where digital threats evolve rapidly, every citizen plays a vital role in protecting personal and national cybersecurity. This portal provides essential resources to help you understand emerging risks and adopt protective measures.

Recent Trends

Phishing attacks have become increasingly sophisticated, with criminals using AI-generated content to impersonate trusted institutions. Ransomware incidents targeting both individuals and businesses have surged, often disguised as routine emails or software updates. Identity theft cases involving stolen personal information have multiplied, particularly through compromised social media accounts.

Protective Measures

We strongly urge all citizens to enable multi-factor authentication on important accounts, regularly update software and security patches, and remain vigilant about suspicious communications. Never share sensitive information through unverified channels, and be especially cautious of urgent requests that pressure you to act quickly without verification.

Reporting Incidents

If you suspect you have been targeted or have already become a victim, report the incident immediately through our 24/7 hotline. Quick reporting can prevent further damage and help authorities track emerging threats.

밑줄 친 vigilant의 의미와 가장 가까운 것은?
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 8번(assess=evaluate), 2025년 지방직 8번 / 핵심 개념: 공통 지문 시사 어휘] 정답은 ④번. 'remain vigilant about suspicious communications(의심스러운 통신에 대해 경계를 유지하라)'에서 vigilant는 '경계하는, 방심하지 않는'의 의미로 alert(경계하는)와 동의어. indifferent(무관심한)는 반대. exhausted(지친)는 맥락에 부적합. neutral(중립적인)은 보안 경계와 무관. vigilant는 보안·범죄 예방 시사 영어에서 빈출 어휘.

questions.question_text: 윗글의 목적으로 가장 적절한 것은? (8번 공통지문 문제)

choices.text: to inform citizens about cyber threats and protective measures
to announce new criminal laws on cybercrime
to recruit cybersecurity professionals for the government

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 9번(to explain how to file complaints), 2025년 지방직 9번 / 핵심 개념: 공통 지문 글의 목적 - 정부 안내문] 정답은 ①번. 본문 'This portal provides essential resources to help you understand emerging risks and adopt protective measures(신흥 위험 이해 및 보호 조치 채택을 돕는 필수 자원 제공)'에서 명확. 위협 인식 + 보호 조치 + 신고 방법을 모두 안내하므로 '시민에게 사이버 위협과 보호 조치를 알리는 것'이 목적. 상업 제품 광고가 아닌 공공 안내. 새 법률 발표가 아닌 인식 제고. 인력 채용 내용은 없음.

questions.question_text:
다음 글의 목적으로 가장 적절한 것은?

[이메일]
To: ministry-environment@gov.kr
From: hjlee@greengroup.org
Date: April 5, 2026
Subject: Public Consultation Request - Carbon Neutrality Plan

Dear Minister of Environment,

I am writing on behalf of the Green Citizens Alliance, a coalition of 47 environmental organizations representing over 500,000 members nationwide. We are reaching out regarding the recently announced 2050 Carbon Neutrality Implementation Plan.

While we strongly support the government's commitment to carbon neutrality, we have significant concerns about the limited opportunities for public input during the plan's development. The current consultation period of only two weeks is insufficient for meaningful civil society engagement on policies that will fundamentally reshape our nation's energy systems and economy.

We respectfully request that the Ministry:
1. Extend the public consultation period to at least 60 days
2. Hold regional town hall meetings in major metropolitan areas
3. Establish a formal advisory committee including civil society representatives
4. Publish detailed economic impact assessments before finalizing the plan

These measures would significantly enhance the legitimacy and effectiveness of the final implementation plan. We believe that genuine public engagement leads to better policy outcomes and stronger public support for ambitious climate action.

We would welcome the opportunity to discuss these recommendations in person at your earliest convenience.

Respectfully,
Hyejin Lee
Executive Director, Green Citizens Alliance

choices.text: to oppose the government's carbon neutrality goals
to request expanded public consultation on the climate plan
to announce a new environmental organization
to apply for government funding for climate research

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 11번(to solicit the Council), 2026년 국가직 10번 / 핵심 개념: 시사 이메일 글의 목적 - 정책 의견 수렴 요청] 정답은 ②번. 'We respectfully request that the Ministry: 1. Extend the public consultation period to at least 60 days(공론화 기간 60일로 연장 요청)' 등 네 가지 구체적 요구사항을 제시하며 핵심은 '공론화 확대 요청'. 'we strongly support the government's commitment(정부 의지에 강력 지지)'이므로 반대가 아닌 절차 개선 요청. 새 단체 발표가 아니라 기존 연합의 의견 제출. 연구비 신청 내용 없음. 신유형: 시민단체-정부 정책 협의는 거버넌스 시사 어휘.

questions.question_text:
다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

[안내문]
DIGITAL LITERACY WORKSHOP FOR SENIORS

Free workshops to help older adults navigate the digital world with confidence.

WHO
Residents aged 65 and older
All skill levels welcome, from complete beginners to those with some experience

WHAT
Learn essential digital skills including:
- Smartphone basics and useful apps
- Online banking and government services
- Video calls with family members
- Recognizing online scams and protecting personal information
- Health management apps and telemedicine

WHEN
Every Tuesday and Thursday, 10 AM - 12 PM
Program runs from May 5 to July 25
No class on national holidays

WHERE
Community Senior Center, 2nd floor
45 Hope Avenue

WHAT TO BRING
Participants may bring their own devices, but tablets and smartphones will be provided for those who don't have one.

REGISTRATION
Pre-registration required. Call 1588-1234 or visit the Senior Center office. Limited to 20 participants per session to ensure personalized attention.

choices.text: 사전 등록 없이 자유롭게 참여할 수 있다.

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 14번, 2025년 지방직 12번 / 핵심 개념: 안내문 내용 일치 - 등록 요건 함정] 정답은 ③번. 'Pre-registration required(사전 등록 필수)' 및 'Limited to 20 participants per session(세션당 20명 제한)'으로 자유 참여 불가. 'Residents aged 65 and older'와 일치. 'tablets and smartphones will be provided for those who don't have one'과 일치. 'No class on national holidays'와 일치. 신유형: 노인 디지털 격차 해소는 시사 빈출 주제.

questions.question_text:
다음 글에서 The Sustainable Cities Network에 대한 내용과 일치하는 것은?

[웹페이지: scn.org]

The Sustainable Cities Network

The Sustainable Cities Network (SCN) is a global coalition of municipalities working together to address urgent urban challenges including climate change, social inequality, and infrastructure resilience. Founded in 2010, our network has grown from 12 founding members to over 200 cities across six continents.

What We Do

- Knowledge Exchange:
We facilitate peer-to-peer learning through regular conferences, online platforms, and city-to-city partnerships. Successful programs in one city can be adapted and implemented in others.

- Technical Assistance:
Member cities receive expert support on policy design, project implementation, and impact measurement. Our specialists have backgrounds in urban planning, environmental science, and public administration.

- Advocacy at International Forums:
SCN represents the collective voice of cities at UN climate conferences and other international gatherings, ensuring that urban perspectives shape global policy.

- Research and Publications:
We publish annual reports analyzing urban trends, best practices, and emerging challenges to inform policymakers and the public.

Membership is open to municipalities of all sizes that commit to our sustainability principles. Annual membership fees scale according to city population.

choices.text: All membership fees are identical regardless of city size.
It started with over 200 founding member cities.
Its members are limited to large metropolitan areas only.
It represents cities at international policy forums.

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 15번, 2025년 지방직 11번 / 핵심 개념: 기관 소개 내용 일치] 정답은 ④번. 'SCN represents the collective voice of cities at UN climate conferences and other international gatherings(국제 회의에서 도시들의 집합적 목소리 대변)'과 일치. 'grown from 12 founding members to over 200 cities'이므로 시작은 12개 도시. 'Membership is open to municipalities of all sizes(모든 규모 자치단체 가입 가능)'와 반대. 'Annual membership fees scale according to city population(도시 인구에 따라 회비 차등)'과 반대. 신유형: 국제 도시 네트워크는 글로벌 거버넌스 시사 영역.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 옳지 않은 것을 고르시오.

The rise of cryptocurrency ①has prompted regulators worldwide to reconsider how digital assets should be classified and taxed. While some countries have embraced these technologies, ②other have imposed strict restrictions on their use. Central banks are now exploring ③whether they should issue their own digital currencies, a development that could fundamentally transform how money ④functions in modern economies.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 지방직 13번 / 핵심 개념: 어법 밑줄 - other vs others] 정답은 ①번. 'other'는 형용사로 명사 앞에 와야 하므로 단독으로 쓸 수 없음. 여기서는 '다른 나라들'을 의미하므로 대명사 'others'가 와야 함. has prompted는 현재완료로 동사 정상. whether는 '~인지 아닌지' 명사절 접속사로 explore의 목적어절을 이끔. functions는 단수 주어 money와 수일치. 'other / others / the other / the others' 차이는 어법 단골 함정. 신유형 주제: 가상화폐 규제.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 옳지 않은 것을 고르시오.

The pandemic ①accelerated the adoption of remote work technologies, fundamentally changing how organizations operate. Companies that once required all employees ②work from physical offices have now embraced flexible arrangements. While productivity in many sectors has remained stable or even increased, concerns about employee mental health, team cohesion, and career development opportunities ③have prompted ongoing debate about ④what the optimal balance should be.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2026년 국가직 14번, 2024년 국가직 7번 / 핵심 개념: 어법 밑줄 - require + 목적어 + to부정사] 정답은 ②번. 'require(요구하다)' 동사는 'require + 목적어 + to부정사' 형태를 취하므로 'required all employees to work from physical offices'가 정확. 'work'를 'to work'로 고쳐야 함. accelerated는 단순 과거 동사로 적절. have prompted는 복수 주어 concerns에 수일치한 현재완료. what은 의문사로 'should be의 보어 절'을 이끎(should be 다음 보어 자리 비어있음). require/ask/expect/want/allow/enable은 모두 to부정사 동사.

questions.question_text:
다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?

The concept of 'digital divide' has evolved significantly since the term was first coined in the 1990s. Initially, the divide referred to a simple binary: who had access to the internet and who did not. As internet access has become nearly universal in developed nations, researchers now distinguish between three levels of digital inequality. The first level concerns physical access to devices and connectivity. The second level addresses digital skills—the ability to effectively use technologies for meaningful purposes. The third and increasingly important level focuses on outcomes: even among those with equal access and skills, some groups derive greater social, economic, and educational benefits from technology than others. These tangible outcomes are shaped by broader social inequalities, including educational background, family resources, and professional networks. Understanding this three-level framework reveals that simply providing internet access or basic training cannot fully address digital inequality. Effective policy must consider how technology interacts with existing social structures to either reduce or reinforce disparities in opportunity.

choices.text: comparing digital skills between developing and developed nations
the history of internet development in the 1990s
evolving understanding of digital inequality beyond simple access
successful policies for expanding internet infrastructure

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 지방직 15번, 2026년 국가직 15번 / 핵심 개념: 시사 주제 - 디지털 격차의 복잡성] 정답은 ③번. 글 전체가 '디지털 격차' 개념의 진화를 추적: 단순 접속(1990s) → 3단계 격차(접속/기술/성과)로 확장. 핵심은 '접속을 넘어선 디지털 불평등의 진화된 이해'. 1990년대 인터넷 발전사는 도입의 일부일 뿐. 인프라 확장 정책은 글이 비판하는 '부족한 접근'. 개도국-선진국 비교는 글의 초점이 아닌 발달 단계의 일례. 신유형: 디지털 격차의 다차원성은 시사 빈출.

questions.question_text:
주어진 문장이 들어갈 위치로 가장 적절한 것은?

This silent process can have measurable impacts on biodiversity, food production, and human health.

When people think of pollution, they typically imagine smokestacks belching dark clouds or oil slicks coating beaches. ( ① ) But some of the most consequential forms of pollution are invisible to the naked eye. ( ② ) Microplastic particles smaller than a grain of rice now permeate oceans, soils, and even the air we breathe. ( ③ ) Studies have detected microplastics in human blood, lungs, and even placentas, though the long-term health effects remain under investigation. ( ④ ) Addressing invisible pollution requires equally sophisticated monitoring systems and regulations that can target what cannot be easily seen.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 16번, 2025년 지방직 17번 / 핵심 개념: 문장 삽입 - 지시어(This silent process)] 정답은 ④번. 주어진 문장 'This silent process(이 조용한 과정)'은 앞에서 언급된 '눈에 보이지 않는 미세 오염 과정'을 가리킴. 앞에서 미세플라스틱이 바다·토양·공기에 퍼져 있다는 '조용한 과정'을 설명. 자리에 삽입하면 '이 조용한 과정이 생물다양성·식량·건강에 영향'으로 자연 연결되고, 다음 문장에서 인체 영향의 구체 증거(혈액·폐·태반)를 제시하는 흐름과 부합. 에 넣으면 'Addressing'으로 시작하는 결론 직전에 끼어 어색. 신유형: 미세플라스틱·환경오염은 시사 빈출.

questions.question_text:
다음 글의 흐름상 어색한 문장은?

The transition to renewable energy is accelerating worldwide, driven by falling costs, technological improvements, and increasing climate concerns. ①Solar and wind power, once considered too expensive for mainstream adoption, are now the cheapest sources of new electricity generation in most countries. ②Battery storage technology has advanced rapidly, addressing the intermittency challenge that historically limited renewable deployment. ③Many traditional energy companies are facing significant financial pressure as investors increasingly favor sustainable alternatives. ④Coal-fired power plants continue to be the most economically efficient way to generate electricity in industrial economies, despite their environmental impact. Governments worldwide are now implementing policies designed to phase out fossil fuels while supporting the rapid expansion of clean energy infrastructure.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 17번, 2024년 지방직 16번 / 핵심 개념: 흐름상 어색한 문장 - 사실 역행] 정답은 ①번. 글 전체가 '재생에너지 전환 가속'을 주장하는데, 'Coal-fired power plants continue to be the most economically efficient(석탄 발전이 가장 경제적이라고 주장)'으로 의 'Solar and wind power are now the cheapest sources(태양·풍력이 가장 저렴)'와 정면으로 모순. 또한 다음 문장 'phase out fossil fuels(화석연료 단계적 폐지)'와도 충돌. 모두 재생에너지 전환의 동력을 보강. 신유형: 에너지 전환은 시사 단골 주제.

questions.question_text:
주어진 글 다음에 이어질 글의 순서로 가장 적절한 것은?

Misinformation has become one of the defining challenges of the digital age, with profound implications for democracy, public health, and social cohesion.

(A) Researchers have identified several psychological factors that make humans particularly susceptible to false information. Confirmation bias leads us to accept claims that align with existing beliefs while critically examining those that contradict them. Emotional content, especially fear or outrage, spreads faster than neutral information.

(B) However, the digital environment has transformed misinformation from a localized problem into a global phenomenon. Social media platforms can spread false claims to millions within hours, often faster than corrections can follow. Algorithms designed to maximize engagement frequently amplify provocative or sensational content.

(C) Of course, misinformation itself is not new—rumors and propaganda have existed throughout human history. Earlier eras saw deliberate disinformation campaigns during wars, political movements, and commercial competitions.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 18번, 2025년 지방직 16번 / 핵심 개념: 글의 순서 - 역사→현대 변화→심리 기제] 정답은 ②번. 주어진 글: 디지털 시대 허위정보 도전 소개. (C): 'Of course, misinformation itself is not new(허위정보 자체는 새롭지 않다)'로 역사적 맥락 제공(어조 양보). (B): 'However, the digital environment has transformed misinformation(그러나 디지털 환경이 변화시켰다)'으로 (C)의 양보를 받아 현대의 차이점 제시. (A): 'Researchers have identified several psychological factors(연구자들이 심리적 요인을 규명)'로 디지털 환경에서 왜 인간이 취약한지 심리 기제 설명. 따라서 (C)→(B)→(A). 신유형: 허위정보 시사 분야.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

The rapid development of artificial intelligence raises profound questions about the future of human work and meaning. Optimists argue that AI will free humans from tedious tasks, allowing us to focus on creative, interpersonal, and ethically significant work. Pessimists warn of mass unemployment and widening inequality as machines take over jobs across multiple sectors. Both views, however, share a common assumption that may need examination: that the value of human work lies primarily in its economic output. Throughout history, work has provided humans not just with income but with identity, social connection, and a sense of contribution to something larger than ourselves. If AI eventually performs most economically productive tasks more efficiently than humans can, societies will face questions that extend beyond simple economics. We will need to consider how people find _____________ in a world where their economic contribution is no longer essential. This challenge may require us to fundamentally rethink the relationship between work, value, and human flourishing.

choices.text: more efficient ways to compete with machines
purpose and meaning in their lives

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 19번, 2025년 지방직 19번 / 핵심 개념: 시사 빈칸 추론 - 인간 노동의 의미] 정답은 ③번. 글의 핵심 명제는 '노동의 가치 = 경제적 산출이 아닌 정체성·연결·기여(identity, social connection, sense of contribution)'. 빈칸은 'in a world where their economic contribution is no longer essential(경제적 기여가 더 이상 필수가 아닌 세계)'에서 찾아야 할 것이므로 '삶의 목적과 의미(purpose and meaning)'가 정답. 또한 마지막 문장 'rethink the relationship between work, value, and human flourishing(노동·가치·인간 번영의 관계 재고)'과도 호응. 임금·복지는 글이 비판하는 경제적 환원주의. 기술 스킬도 경제적 가치 차원. 기계와 효율 경쟁은 글의 취지와 정반대.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

The concept of 'planetary boundaries' provides a scientific framework for understanding the limits within which humanity can safely operate. Identified by researchers at the Stockholm Resilience Centre, these boundaries include climate change, biodiversity loss, freshwater use, land system change, and several other interconnected systems. Crossing any of these thresholds risks triggering abrupt and potentially irreversible changes in Earth's systems. What makes this framework particularly powerful is its recognition that environmental challenges _____________. Climate change interacts with biodiversity loss; forest destruction affects both carbon storage and water cycles; ocean acidification threatens marine food webs that humans depend on. This interconnection means that solving one environmental problem often requires addressing others simultaneously, and that progress in one area can sometimes undermine progress in another. Recognizing these complex relationships demands integrated policy approaches rather than fragmented solutions targeting individual issues in isolation.

choices.text: have been completely solved by recent technology
depend solely on individual consumer choices
exist in isolation from each other
are interconnected and cannot be addressed separately

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 지방직 20번, 2026년 국가직 20번 / 핵심 개념: 시사 빈칸 추론 - 환경 시스템의 상호연결성] 정답은 ④번. 빈칸 직후 'Climate change interacts with biodiversity loss; forest destruction affects both carbon storage and water cycles(기후변화-생물다양성, 산림-탄소-수문이 상호작용)'으로 구체 예시. 또한 'This interconnection means that solving one environmental problem often requires addressing others simultaneously(한 문제 해결은 다른 문제와 동시 대응 필요)'에서 명확. 따라서 '상호 연결되어 있고 별도로 다룰 수 없다'가 정답. 고립되어 있다는 것은 정반대. 완전히 해결되었다는 것은 사실 오류. 개인 선택에만 의존한다는 것은 글의 'integrated policy approaches(통합 정책 접근)' 강조와 반대. 신유형: 행성적 한계 framework은 환경 거버넌스 최신 시사.
